page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.large is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor based on the x86_64 architecture, operating at a base frequency of 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs, providing a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is not bundled with this instance, and it does not include any GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate that this instance sits in the lower tier of modern cloud compute resources. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance is measured at 1099.58, while the multi-core score reaches 1459.48. Memory bandwidth tests show 81.52 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 10.38 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 9.23 GB/sec for large-block writes. Geekbench results reflect this positioning, with a single-core score of 860.0 and a multi-core score of 1052.0. In application-level tests, the instance achieves 1.37 million operations per second on Redis SET workloads and a static web serving throughput of 1.22 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.se1ne.large offers a dedicated CPU allocation and a generous memory footprint relative to its compute capacity, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high CPU throughput. However, the trade-off is its low processing speed and limited memory bandwidth. This instance is best suited for lightweight database operations, caching layers, basic web serving, and memory-bound utility tasks that can operate within a 1 Gbps network baseline.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor at 2.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and KVM hypervisor, it provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, and operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark results place its compute and memory performance in the lower tier, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 1052.0 and uncached memory read bandwidth of 10.38 GB/sec. Consequently, this instance represents a qualitative trade-off, offering dedicated memory capacity at a lower performance profile. It is best suited for lightweight memory-bound workloads, small database operations, caching layers, and basic web serving.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.large is a memory-optimized instance with 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 1 Gbps network baseline, designed for lightweight memory-bound workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.large features 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 1 Gbps bandwidth for memory-optimized, lightweight workloads.
tagline: A dedicated dual-core instance featuring a high memory-to-CPU ratio for lightweight, memory-bound applications and basic caching workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Suited for lightweight database operations and caching layers
Optimized for memory-bound tasks with low CPU requirements
